most_recent_game.recap_text:
CHAPEL HILL, N.C. –
A five-run eighth proved to be the undoing for the University of Louisville baseball team in a 7-6 loss at No. 9 North Carolina on Sunday in Chapel Hill, N.C.
Louisville (16-8, 3-3 ACC) held a 4-2 lead heading into the eighth inning before letting the advantage slip away.
Jack Brown
retired the first two batters of the eighth on two pitches before issuing a walk and giving up a single into left and exiting the contest. A hit by pitch and a walk forced home the first run before a single on the infield allowed the tying run to score.
North Carolina (20-4-1, 6-3) then delivered the biggest swing of the game on a bases clearing double to left-centerfield, just out of the reach of
Griffin Crain
to give the Tar Heels a 7-4 lead.
The Cardinals threatened in the ninth, getting a two-run homer from
Tague Davis
to bring them back within one.
Bayram Hot
followed with a single to put the tying run on with just one out.
Ben Slanker
then hit a ball back through the middle that had a chance of getting through, but the UNC second baseman made a diving stop and throw to retire him for the second out. A first pitch groundout then put the finishing touches on the loss.
North Carolina took the early lead with a run in the second but the Cardinals responded in each of the next two innings.
Kade Elam
cashed in
Kyle Campbell's
leadoff double with an RBI in the third to tie the game, and Campbell hit a roller on the infield that scored Hot one inning later to put the Cards in front.
Jimmy Nugent
then dropped a single into right field that plated
Jax Hisle
and stretched the lead to two.
The Tar Heels cut it back to one on a solo homer in the fifth, but the Cards pushed it back to two in the seventh on a pinch-hit RBI single from Slanker.
Colton Hartman
drew his first weekend start of the year on Sunday, allowing one run over 3.1 innings.
Davis was 3-for-5 on the day for the Cards, hitting his 13
th
homer of the season. His two RBIs boosted his total to 50 on the year. Hot and Nugent each tallied two hits.
Louisville will be on the road again on Tuesday night as it travels to Bowling Green to take on Western Kentucky. First pitch is scheduled for 7 p.m. ET.
FOLLOW LOUISVILLE BASEBALL ON SOCIAL MEDIA
Fans can follow Louisville baseball on
X
,
Facebook
or
Instagram
at @LouisvilleBSB.
